line number: TYPE (DEFAULT VALUE) - DESCRIPTION - NOTES FROM DEMO_PIPELINE_CNMFE.PY
frame rate: float (30) - imaging rate in frames per second - movie frame rate
decay_time: float (0.4) - length of typical transient in seconds - length of a typical transient in seconds
dims: (int, int) (computed from fnames) - dimensions of the FOV in pixels -
update_background_components: bool (True) - whether to update the spatial background components - sometimes setting to False improve the results
method_deconvolution: 'oasis'|'cvxpy'|'oasis' ('oasis') - method for solving the constrained deconvolution problem ('oasis', 'cvx', or 'cvxpy') if method cvxpy, primary and secondary (if problem unfeasible for approx solution) - could use 'cvxpy' alternatively
method_init: 'greedy_roi'|'corr_pnr'|'sparse_NMF'|'local_NMF' ('greedy_roi') - initialization method. use 'corr_pnr' for 1p processing and 'sparse_NMF' for dendritic processing - use this for 1 photon
K: int (30) - number of components to be found (per patch or whole FOV depending on whether rf=None) - upper bound on number of components per patch, in general None for 1p data
gSig: [int, int] ([5, 5]) - IMPORTANT radius of average neurons (in pixels) - gaussian width of a 2D gaussian kernel, which approximates a neuron
gSiz: [int, int] ([int(round((x * 2) + 1)) for x in gSig]) - IMPORTANT half-size of bounding box for each neuron - average diameter of a neuron, in general 4*gSig+1
tsub: float (2) - temporal downsampling factor - downsampling factor in time for initialization, increase if you have memory problems -
ssub: float (2) - spatial downsampling factor - downsampling factor in space for initialization, increase if you have memory problems. you can pass them here as boolean vectors -
ssub_B: float (2) - downsampling factor for background during corr_pnr - additional downsampling factor in space for background
ring_size_factor: float (1.5) - radius of ring (*gSig) for computing background during corr_pnr - radius of ring is gSiz*ring_size_factor
min_corr: float (0.85) - IMPORTANT minimum value of correlation image for determining a candidate component during corr_pnr - min peak value from correlation image
min_pnr: float (20) - IMPORTANT minimum value of psnr image for determining a candidate component during corr_pnr - min peak to noise ration from PNR image
normalize_init: bool (True) - whether to equalize the movies during initialization - just leave as is
center_psf: bool (False) - whether to use 1p data processing mode. Set to true for 1p - leave as is for 1 photon
nb: int (1) - number of background components - number of background components (rank) if positive, else exact ring model with following settings: gnb= 0: Return background as b and W; gnb=-1: Return full rank background B; gnb<-1: Don't return background
p: int (2) - order of AR indicator dynamics - order of the autoregressive system
del_duplicates: bool (False) - Delete duplicate components in the overlaping regions between neighboring patches. If False, then merging is used - whether to remove duplicates from initialization
border_pix: int (0) - Number of pixels to exclude around each border - number of pixels to not consider in the borders (Note from Eric: this is computed in the demo from the size of the maximum shift in the motion correction)
low_rank_background: bool (True) - Whether to update the background using a low rank approximation. If False all the nonzero elements of the background components are updated using hals (to be used with one background per patch) - None leaves background of each patch intact, True performs global low-rank approximation if gnb>0
rf: int or None (None) - Half-size of patch in pixels. If None, no patches are constructed and the whole FOV is processed jointly - half-size of the patches in pixels. e.g., if rf=40, patches are 80x80
stride: int or None (None) - Overlap between neighboring patches in pixels - amount of overlap between the patches in pixels (keep it at least large as gSiz, i.e 4 times the neuron size gSig)
only_init: bool (True) - whether to run only the initialization - set it to True to run CNMF-E
nb_patch: int (1) - Number of (local) background components per patch - number of background components (rank) per patch if gnb>0, else it is set automatically
min_SNR: float (2.5) - trace SNR threshold. Traces with SNR above this will get accepted - adaptive way to set threshold on the transient size
rval_thr: float (0.8) - space correlation threshold. Components with correlation higher than this will get accepted - threshold on space consistency (if you lower more components will be accepted, potentially with worst quality)
use_cnn: bool (True) - flag for using the CNN classifier -
merge_thr: float (0.8) - Trace correlation threshold for merging two components - merging threshold, max correlation allowed
pw_rigid: bool (False) - flag for performing pw-rigid motion correction - flag for pw-rigid motion correction
max_shifts: (int, int) ((6, 6)) - maximum shifts per dimension in pixels - maximum allowed rigid shift
gSig_filt: int or None (None) - size of kernel for high pass spatial filtering in 1p data. If None no spatial filtering is performed - size of filter, in general gSig (see below), change this one if algorithm does not work
strides: (int, int) ((96, 96)) - how often to start a new patch in pw-rigid registration. Size of each patch will be strides + overlaps - start a new patch for pw-rigid motion correction every x pixels
overlaps: (int, int) ((24, 24)) - overlap between patches in pixels in pw-rigid motion correction - overlap between pathes (size of patch strides + overlaps). maximum deviation allowed for patch with respect to rigid shifts
max_deviation_rigid: int (3) - maximum deviation in pixels between rigid shifts and shifts of individual patches -
border_nan: bool or str ('copy') - flag for allowing NaN in the boundaries. True allows NaN, whereas 'copy' copies the value of the nearest data point -
